text: Among the Ashworth graves, one is unmarked. Scraping away moss reveals a single word: 'FORGIVE.'
You look into the well water. For a moment — just a moment — you see a face that isn't yours looking back. It mouths a word you can't hear. Then it's gone.
Through the thin walls of Elara's cottage, you hear her humming. A lullaby. Old, wordless, and unbearably sad. She stops when she realizes someone might be listening.
A coin wedged into a crack in the alley wall. On one side, the Guild's hollow hand. On the other, scratched crudely: a child's drawing of a house. A tally mark beneath it. Fourteen marks.

Brother Maren kneels at the altar, alone. But he isn't praying in the Church's words. He's whispering: 'Father. Father, I'm sorry. I came back and I made it worse. I'm sorry.' He doesn't know you're there.

Among the campfire ashes, a piece of parchment. A sketch of a village — not Ashvale. Another village, from another time. 'Rivendale' is written below in Voss's handwriting. The village she couldn't save.

A covered mirror in the lord's bedroom. You lift the cloth. The mirror is cracked — smashed by a fist. In the fractured reflection, Ashworth's face is multiplied. Each fragment shows a different expression. Fear. Guilt. Rage. Resignation. All of them are him.

Deep in the forest, you hear singing. Not words — a melody carried by wind through leaves. It sounds like a lullaby. The same lullaby Elara hums. The forest remembers Silas's voice. It remembers everything.
